Our dinner menu is bold and flavor-forward, featuring dishes inspired by Pacific Rim cities like Tokyo, Hong Kong, and Baja. From yakitori skewers charred to perfection to Hong Kong chicken sliders, our vegan Korean BBQ tofu bowl, and crispy gyoza tacos, there's a touch of comfort and creativity in every dish. Expect a balance of vibrant, herbaceous, and umami-rich plates—paired with classic, thoughtfully crafted cocktails that nod to coastal Asia and the West Coast of North America.
See a PDF of our dinner menu
here
.

Available every Saturday and Sunday from 10:30 AM-2:30 PM, our brunch menu brings a Pacific Rim twist to Virginia Highland. Highlights include chicken and bubble waffles, a bao benedict with smoked gouda fondue, and spicy tuna “toast” on crispy hash browns. Whether you lean sweet, savory, or vegetarian, there’s something for every brunch mood—paired with frozen cocktails, mimosas, Thai basil palomas, or shaken coffees.
See a PDF of our brunch menu
here
.

RECENT REVIEW: BRUNCH
Scott A. on
July 22, 2025
Whiskey Bird may be one of the most universally beloved restaurants on North Highland.
Anthony and Chad along with executive chef Joshua put together an exceptional mix of vibrant plates from all over the world.
Unlike some other restaurants, you are not squeezed tight to achieve additional table count.
The atmosphere is refreshing thanks to the friendly team and the artistry on the walls.
The brunch menu rivals that of any in the city. Where to start?  That’s easy, okonomiyaki loaded Japanese street fries.
From there, dig into the Frittata Yada-Yada or the Bao Benedict for a perfect Sunday morning brunch.
In the summer, on a busy Sunday morning, the folks are clutching glasses of wine, waiting for their table and enjoying a lovely Sunday morning conversation. Have they dined at Whiskey Bird before? Yes. And they’ll be dining with Whiskey Bird again and again.
Highly recommended
Available every Friday, our lunch menu hits that perfect midday balance—fresh, filling, and full of flavor. We take our salads seriously, stacking them with fresh veggies, ingredients like avocado, sunflower seeds, and cotija cheese, and bold house-made dressings. You’ll get a taste of our popular brunch items too, but the chicken and bao (our spin on chicken and biscuits), the fried egg sammie, and the BLTA club with gochujang ranch are lunch menu exclusives.
See a PDF of our lunch menu
here
.
